evals.prompt: We're a B2B SaaS for restaurants ($79/month). We have 500 paying customers. Most came through word of mouth but we don't have a formal referral program. How should we design one?
evals.expected_output: Referral program design including: incentive structure (double-sided vs. one-sided), reward type (discount vs. cash vs. credit), referral mechanics, messaging templates, and launch plan.
assertions: Recommends double-sided referral (both referrer and referred get reward)
Addresses reward type options (account credit likely best for B2B SaaS)
Covers referral mechanics (unique link, code, or email)
Provides messaging for the referral ask (email, in-app prompt)
Addresses when to ask (after value milestone, not on day 1)

evals.prompt: Our referral program has a 2% participation rate after 6 months. How do we improve this?
evals.expected_output: Referral program audit covering: incentive attractiveness, ask timing, program visibility, friction in the referral process, and A/B test hypotheses.
assertions: Assesses whether the incentive is compelling enough
Covers ask timing (when in customer journey)
Addresses program visibility (do customers know it exists?)
Provides specific A/B test hypotheses to improve participation
Notes that 2% is below average and identifies likely root causes

evals.prompt: Set up referral tracking in our app
evals.expected_output: Offers referral program strategy guidance, notes that technical implementation (tracking) requires engineering work, and recommends referral platforms (ReferralHero, Viral Loops, etc.).
assertions: Notes that tracking implementation is engineering work
Recommends specific referral program platforms as options
